titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Martha McPhee Papers
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 16334312.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday April 7, 2023 at 03:11

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: In English. Some materials in Italian.

did.abstract: Notebooks, journals, writings, correspondence, personal papers, and printed material, as well as computer and audiovisual media, by or relating to writer Martha McPhee. Collection materials document McPhee's writing process, as well as her relationships with her family including her sister Laura McPhee, mother Pryde Brown, father John McPhee, husband Mark Svenvold, and others; her experience in a blended family; her childhood near Princeton, New Jersey; her trips to Italy in her youth; and her adult life in New York City.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 48-49 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 50-53 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 54-55: Restricted until after the death of John McPhee. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 56: Restricted until January 1, 2060. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Chris Calhoun Agency on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: I. Notebooks and Journals, 1972-2019. II. Writings, 1976-2019. III. Correspondence, 1970-2019. IV. Personal Papers, circa 1906-2011. V. Printed Material, 1910-2013. VI. Computer and Audiovisual Media, circa 1996-2011. VIII. Restricted Papers.
Born-digital materials are arranged as a single format subseries within Series VI. Computer and Audiovisual Media, and original order has been maintained. No further arrangement work has been done.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Martha McPhee (born 1965) is an American novelist. She is the daughter of literary journalist John McPhee (born 1931) and his first wife photographer Pryde Brown (born 1935). She is also the sister of novelist Jenny McPhee, photographer Laura McPhee, and architectural historian Sarah McPhee. Following Pryde Brown's marriage to psychologist Dan Sullivan in 1969, the McPhee children grew up on a farm outside of Princeton, New Jersey, with their younger half-sister, Joan Sullivan, and their stepfather's children from his first marriage.

McPhee graduated with a Bachelor of Arts from Bowdoin College in 1987 and received her Master of Fine Arts in Fiction from Columbia University in 1997. She received a National Endowment for the Arts grant to complete her first novel, Bright Angel Time (1997). Her second novel, Gorgeous Lies (2002), was a finalist for the National Book Award. Her other novels include L'America (2006), Dear Money (2010), and An Elegant Woman (2020). She has been a professor in the English Department at Hofstra University since 2002.
McPhee married poet and writer Mark Svenvold (born 1958) in 1996. The couple and their two children, Ana Livia Svenvold McPhee and Jasper Svenvold McPhee, live in New York City.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Martha McPhee Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

Computer files were accessioned by the Yale University Digital Accessioning Support Service (DASS). Born digital archival material acquired on media by Yale University Library repositories is accessioned through the DASS prior to researcher use. For more information on the DASS and the actions taken during its accessioning work, click here: Born Digital @ Yale: Digital Accessioning Support Service.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebooks, journals, writings, correspondence, personal papers, and printed material, as well as computer and audiovisual media, by or relating to writer Martha McPhee. Collection materials document McPhee's writing process, as well as her relationships with her family including her sister Laura McPhee, mother Pryde Brown, father John McPhee, husband Mark Svenvold, and others; her experience in a blended family; her childhood near Princeton, New Jersey; her trips to Italy in her youth; and her adult life in New York City.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Martha McPhee Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains 46 notebooks and journals kepy bt Martha McPhee.

The earliest journals contain school exercises and short stories. Beginning in 1980, journals document McPhee's travels in Europe, her friendship with Donatella "Dodi" Trotti, her relationship with her family, her interactions with boys, and her desire to write.

Journals kept in 1989 and onward document McPhee's application and subsequent enrollment in Columbia University's Master of Fine Arts Program in Fiction, life in New York City, the writing of her first novel Bright Angel Time , and her relationships with family, friends, and romantic partners. The journals also include notes for Gorgeous Lies (under working titles Quo Vadis and Farther India ) and An Elegant Woman .

Journals taper off in the early 2000s and resume in 2016, when McPhee begins to adhere dried flowers, letters, photographs, clippings, and other ephemera to journal pages. The near-daily entries in these seven journals chronicle the writing and revision of An Elegant Woman , as well as McPhee's thoughts on motherhood and her relationship with her father. They also document her class instruction at Hofstra University, the renovation of her New York City apartment, her reaction to the 2016 presidential election, and work on a new novel, Scheherazade . These later journals contain family photographs of her children, Ana Livia Svenvold McPhee and Jasper Svenvold McPhee, as well as of her grandparents and great grandparents.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into three subseries: Drafts, Shorter Works, and Writings of Others.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related materials can be found in Series VI: Computer and Audiovisual Media.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains drafts and draft fragments, notes, research materials, page proofs, publicity material, reviews, and other material related to books, articles, essays, and other projects by Martha McPhee. The series also contains works by her husband Mark Svenvold.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains notes, research materials, drafts, publicity and press clippings, reviews, contracts, and correspondence related to McPhee's books. Books include L'America (2006), Bright Angel Time (1997), Dear Money (2010), An Elegant Woman (2020), and Gorgeous Lies (2002).

It also includes contracts and reviews for Martha and Jenny McPhee's translation of John Paul II's Crossing the Threshold of Hope (1995), as well as for Martha, Jenny, and Laura McPhee's Girls: Ordinary Girls and Their Extraordinary Pursuits (2000).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Working titles: I Can't Keep Up: The Truth About The Joneses , I Can't Keep Up

title.part: Girls: Ordinary Girls and Their Extraordinary Pursuits

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Working titles: Quo Vadis , Farther India

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title, followed by untitled material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of drafts and copies pertaining to articles, essays, and other works written by McPhee. There are also research files and contracts.

did.unittitle: "Hawaiian Sojourns: Taking the Lava Trail Up Kilauea"
Summer experience essay for Bowdoin College
"Varanasi Diary: Death and Life on the Ganges"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of contracts, poetry, and reviews by or relating to Mark Svenvold.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is organized into two subseries: Family Correspondence and Other Correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains letters and printed email exchanges between Martha McPhee and family, friends, writers, and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, followed by unidentified material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include McPhee's mother Pryde Brown; grandmother Katheryn T. Brown; sisters Laura McPhee, Jenny McPhee, Sarah McPhee, and Joan Sullivan; stepfather Dan Sullivan; and other Sullivans and extended family members.

Other correspondents include Martha's grandparents Harry McPhee and Mary McPhee; stepmother Yolanda Whitman; stepsisters Katherine Harrop and Vanessa Speir; other McPhees and extended family members; and husband Mark Svenvold.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incoming correspondence for 40th birthday.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, followed by correspondents identified by first name only and unidentified material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include McPhee's friends Christina Mitchell Ball, Kate Keenan, and Donatella "Dodi" Trotti; boyfriends Jacob Kinnard and Giulio Moroni; literary agent Sarah Chalftant; and writers Roger Angell, Richard Appel, Lauren Belfer, Charlie Buck, Stephen Koch, Tom O'Brien, Leila Philip, Mona Simpson, and Lex Williford.
There are also high school class notes exchanged by Martha McPhee, Kate Keenan, Elizabeth H. Golden, "Mark," and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Class notes filed elsewhere in the collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Class notes filed elsewhere in the collection.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series IV is arranged alphabetically by format or content.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series IV contains an address book, agendas, ephemera, financial documents, photographs, scrapbooks, and other papers kept by Martha McPhee.

did.unittitle: Wedding program for Martha McPhee and Mark Svenvold

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes small picture frame, bookjackets, loose papers and fragments, and a 1968 poster for the film Gone With the Wind .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series V is arranged alphabetically by title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series V contains periodicals and other publications featuring the writings of Martha McPhee. Some periodicals feature the writings of Mark Svenvold and other authors.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings and unidentified materials.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 48-49 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 50-53 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series VI is organized into two subseries: Computer Media and Audiovisual Media.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series VI contains computer and audiovisual media including computer files, optical disks, floppy disks, and audiocassettes. Included are interviews, drafts and notes, family movies, and other media by or relating to Martha McPhee.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The title of each disk is derived from the handwritten title on the disk label, presumably created by McPhee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of 1 laptop and 8 3.5-inch floppy disks.

The files on the laptop include McPhee's typewritten drafts and notes for Dear Money and An Elegant Woman , as well as for shorter works; publicity materials; photographs; teaching materials; job search materials; invoices and other financial documents; and other personal papers.
The files on the disks include drafts and notes for McPhee's writings (both novels and short stories), as well as teaching materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials on this laptop include McPhee's typewritten drafts and notes for Dear Money and An Elegant Woman , as well as for shorter works; publicity materials; photographs; teaching materials; job search materials; invoices and other financial documents; and other personal papers.
Also present are materials by or relating to Mark Svenvold--chiefly typewritten drafts and notes, as well as teaching materials.

did.physdesc: 1.47 Megabytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material chiefly relates to McPhee's writing and teaching.

did.unittitle: chocolate 1 + 2, Hundred Dollar Days, Letting Yourself Go, Zoetrope 1+2

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material chiefly relates to McPhee's teaching. Also present are outgoing correspondence and writings.

scopecontent.p: Material consists of writings by McPhee. Included are drafts and notes for Gorgeous Lies and An Elegant Woman .
